backlog.title: Sentinel Dedup Bug Fix & Spec Reconciliation
backlog.description: Fix the two Major sentinel-based bugs (duplicate injection when a non-sentinel copy co-loads; sentinel string in user prompt silently disabling all #@ injection) and four Minor spec-deviation issues (F3 magic-number sniff, F5 empty-image handling, Unicode \W lookbehind quirk, assembly format deviation). The core fix replaces the cooperation-based F1 sentinel mechanism with cooperation-independent per-token dedup, simultaneously resolving Issues 1, 2, and 6. The Unicode regex fix addresses Issue 5. Issues 3 and 4 are resolved by documenting the intentional deviations in the README.

milestones.title: Core Dedup Fix & Regex Update (Issues 1, 2, 5, 6)
milestones.description: Replace the F1 sentinel-based dedup with per-token dedup in injectFiles (fixes Issues 1, 2, 6), and update the detection regex to use Unicode-aware word boundaries (fixes Issue 5). These are the code changes; tests ride with each subtask per the implicit TDD workflow.

tasks.title: Per-token dedup replaces sentinel mechanism (Issues 1, 2, 6)
tasks.description: The F1 sentinel dedup is cooperation-based: it only prevents re-injection if the prior copy also stamped the sentinel. When a non-sentinel copy co-loads (e.g. a stale global version), the sentinel guard fails to fire, and every #@file is injected twice. Additionally, the sentinel guard tests the raw user prompt before injection, so any prompt containing the literal sentinel string silently disables ALL #@ injection. The fix: add per-token dedup in injectFiles (skip a resolved path if a <file name="<abs>"> block for it already exists in the text), then remove the sentinel entirely (constant, regex, handler guard, assembly insertion). This restores the exact PRD §6.2 assembly format.

subtasks.title: Add per-token dedup check in injectFiles
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_5aa8724eb506/bugfix/001_ff8b3e05b4f9/architecture/system_context.md § 'Pi Runner Input Chaining' and architecture/code_changes_analysis.md § 'Step 1'. Pi's runner (dist/core/extensions/runner.js:881-920) chains input handlers: each handler receives the PREVIOUS handler's transformed text. When a non-sentinel global copy injects first, the sentinel copy receives text that already contains <file name="<abs>"> blocks but lacks the sentinel string. The #@path token is still in the original prompt text.
2. INPUT: The injectFiles function (sharp-at-file.ts ~line 85) receives (text: string, imagesIn: ImageContent[], ctx: {cwd: string}). The for-loop over text.matchAll(FILE_INJECT_RE) at ~line 96 resolves each token to an abs path via expandTildeAndResolve(token, ctx.cwd) at ~line 98.
3. LOGIC: Inside the for-loop, AFTER resolving abs (line ~98) and BEFORE fs.stat(abs) (line ~101), insert: `if (text.includes('<file name="' + abs + '">')) continue;`. This check scans the text parameter (which is event.text as passed by the handler — it may already contain blocks from a prior extension copy's transform). The string check uses includes() (not regex), so path characters like [ ] . need no escaping. This makes dedup cooperation-independent: it catches injection by ANY prior copy regardless of whether that copy stamped a sentinel.
4. OUTPUT: injectFiles now skips tokens whose absolute path already appears in an existing <file name="<abs>"> block in the text. When ALL tokens are skipped (because a prior copy already injected them), count remains 0 and injectFiles returns {text, images: imagesIn, injected: 0} — the handler returns {action: 'continue'}, preventing re-injection. The function's return type and interface are unchanged.
5. MOCKING: No external services to mock. The dedup check is a pure string comparison on the text parameter. The existing test harness (sharp-at-file.test.mjs) imports the real injectFiles via jiti and can verify this by calling injectFiles on already-injected text and asserting injected===0.
6. DOCS: none — this is an internal logic change with no user-facing/config/API surface change. The dedup is transparent to the user.

subtasks.title: Remove sentinel mechanism entirely
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/code_changes_analysis.md § 'Steps 2-4'. The sentinel mechanism consists of 4 parts: (a) INJECT_SENTINEL constant and SENTINEL_RE regex at ~lines 21-27, (b) the handler guard `if (SENTINEL_RE.test(event.text)) return { action: 'continue' }` at ~line 147 in the factory's input handler, (c) the assembly insertion `${INJECT_SENTINEL}

` in the finalText template at ~line 139, (d) the F1 comment blocks above both. With per-token dedup from P1.M1.T1.S1 in place, the sentinel is fully redundant and must be removed to fix Issue 2 (sentinel-in-prompt false negative) and Issue 6 (assembly format deviation).
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T1.S1 having added the per-token dedup check. The sentinel removal is safe ONLY because per-token dedup now handles re-injection prevention.
3. LOGIC: Remove ALL of the following from sharp-at-file.ts:
   (a) Lines ~21-27: the INJECT_SENTINEL constant ('<!--#@file-injected-->'), the SENTINEL_RE constant (/<!--#@file-injected-->/), and their JSDoc comment block.
   (b) ~Line 147 in the input handler: delete `if (SENTINEL_RE.test(event.text)) return { action: 'continue' };` and its preceding F1 comment.
   (c) ~Line 139: change the assembly from `${text}

---

${INJECT_SENTINEL}

${blocks.join('

')}` to `${text}

---

${blocks.join('

')}` (restores exact PRD §6.2 format). Remove the F1 comment above this line.
4. OUTPUT: sharp-at-file.ts no longer references INJECT_SENTINEL or SENTINEL_RE anywhere. The assembly matches PRD §6.2 exactly: `<original prompt>

---

<block 1>

<block 2>`. The handler no longer has a sentinel guard — the three remaining guards (source==='extension', streamingBehavior==='steer', !text.includes('#@')) are untouched.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The existing test harness F1 test (which feeds already-injected text through the handler expecting 'continue' due to sentinel) will now exercise per-token dedup instead — it should still get 'continue' because injectFiles returns injected===0 when all paths are already present. This test may need updating in P1.M2.T1.S1 to assert per-token dedup rather than sentinel presence.
6. DOCS: none — internal mechanism removal; the assembly format now matches the PRD spec exactly (no user-visible change from the spec's perspective).

tasks.title: Unicode-aware regex boundary (Issue 5)
tasks.description: The detection regex FILE_INJECT_RE = /(^|(?<=\W))#@(\S+)/g uses \W in a non-`u` JS regex, which classifies non-ASCII letters (é, ö, ñ, CJK) as non-word. This causes #@ to trigger mid-word in any non-Latin script (e.g. café#@secret.txt injects, despite #@ being mid-word). The fix adds the `u` flag and uses Unicode property escapes for the word boundary.

subtasks.title: Update FILE_INJECT_RE with Unicode property escapes
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md § 'Unicode Regex Fix' and architecture/code_changes_analysis.md § 'Issue 5'. Verified in Node.js: the proposed regex /(^|(?<![\p{L}\p{N}_]))#@(\S+)/gu correctly blocks café#@secret.txt and 日本語#@file while still matching Review #@a.ts, #@a.ts (start-of-string), and foo#@bar (mid-word, correctly blocked). The `u` flag is required for \p{L} (Unicode Letter) and \p{N} (Unicode Number) property escapes. The negative lookbehind (?<![\p{L}\p{N}_]) means 'not preceded by a Unicode letter, number, or underscore' — the correct Unicode-aware word boundary.
2. INPUT: The module-level constant FILE_INJECT_RE at line ~6 of sharp-at-file.ts: `const FILE_INJECT_RE = /(^|(?<=\W))#@(\S+)/g;`
3. LOGIC: Replace the regex with: `const FILE_INJECT_RE = /(^|(?<![\p{L}\p{N}_]))#@(\S+)/gu;`. The capture group structure is unchanged: group 2 still captures the path token (\S+). The ^ alternation still handles start-of-string (where the lookbehind has no preceding char). No other code changes needed — matchAll(FILE_INJECT_RE) and the m[2] access pattern work identically.
4. OUTPUT: FILE_INJECT_RE now uses Unicode-aware word boundaries. Non-ASCII letters before #@ no longer satisfy the boundary condition. The matchAll iteration, capture groups, and downstream token processing are unaffected.
5. MOCKING: No external services. Verify with the test harness: add test cases for café#@file (no match), 日本語#@file (no match), Review #@a.ts (match), #@a.ts at start (match), foo#@bar (no match).
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md §Syntax subsection ('Where it matches') to note that #@ uses Unicode-aware word boundaries — it does not trigger after non-ASCII letters in any language (é, ö, ñ, CJK, etc.). This rides WITH the work.

milestones.title: Test Harness Coverage Expansion
milestones.description: The existing 28-case harness has critical blind spots: (1) the F1 test only re-feeds through the same sentinel-stamping handler — it never simulates a non-sentinel co-loaded copy; (2) no test covers a user prompt containing the sentinel string; (3) no test covers Unicode boundary behavior. These gaps allowed Issues 1, 2, and 5 to slip through. This milestone updates the harness to cover the new per-token dedup behavior and adds regression tests for all three scenarios.

tasks.title: Update harness for dedup and sentinel-removal behaviors
tasks.description: The F1 test currently codifies sentinel-based behavior (it expects the handler to return 'continue' because SENTINEL_RE matches). With the sentinel removed (P1.M1.T1.S2), this test must be rewritten to verify per-token dedup instead. Additionally, new tests must be added for the co-load non-sentinel scenario (Issue 1) and the sentinel-in-prompt scenario (Issue 2).

subtasks.title: Rewrite F1 test and add co-load + sentinel-in-prompt tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md § 'Test Harness Gaps'. The harness imports the real injectFiles and handler via jiti (same mechanism Pi's loader uses). The F1 test (sharp-at-file.test.mjs ~line 'F1') currently: (a) calls injectFiles to get first-pass text, (b) feeds first.text through the captured handler, (c) expects action==='continue' because SENTINEL_RE matches. With the sentinel removed, the handler no longer has a sentinel guard — but per-token dedup in injectFiles will skip all already-injected paths, returning injected===0, so the handler still returns 'continue'. The test must be updated to verify this path explicitly.
2. INPUT: The test harness file sharp-at-file.test.mjs. The mod object exports injectFiles, default (handler factory), and helper functions. Fixture files exist at TMPDIR (a.ts, b.ts, secret.txt, etc.). The FIX constant is {cwd: TMPDIR}.
3. LOGIC: Make three changes to sharp-at-file.test.mjs:
   (a) REWRITE the F1 test case: Rename to 'per-token dedup prevents re-injection'. Instead of relying on the sentinel, call injectFiles on already-injected text and assert injected===0. Also verify via the handler that action==='continue' (because injectFiles returns injected===0 when all paths are deduped). Assert the text contains exactly ONE <file> block for a.ts (not two).
   (b) ADD a co-load test case ('F1b'): Simulate a non-sentinel prior handler. Since injectFiles no longer stamps a sentinel, simply call injectFiles('Review #@a.ts', [], FIX) to get first-pass output, then call injectFiles(first.text, first.images, FIX) again. Assert second.injected===0 and that first.text === second.text (no additional blocks appended). This directly tests the co-load scenario from Issue 1.
   (c) ADD a sentinel-in-prompt test case ('F2'): Create a prompt containing the literal string '<!--#@file-injected-->' AND a valid #@token (e.g. '<!--#@file-injected--> Review #@a.ts'). Call injectFiles and assert injected===1 (the file IS injected — the sentinel string no longer gates injection). Verify via the handler that action==='transform'.
4. OUTPUT: The harness has 3 additional/updated test cases. All must pass with the fixed sharp-at-file.ts. The total case count increases from 28 to ~30.
5. MOCKING: No external services. All tests use the same fixture files and injectFiles/handler pattern as existing tests.
6. DOCS: none — test harness changes have no user-facing documentation surface.

tasks.title: Add Unicode regex boundary regression tests
tasks.description: No existing test covers the Unicode \W lookbehind quirk. Add regression tests verifying that #@ does not trigger after non-ASCII letters, while existing ASCII patterns still work correctly.

subtasks.title: Add Unicode boundary test cases
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md § 'Unicode Regex Fix' for the verified Node.js regex behavior table. The proposed regex /(^|(?<![\p{L}\p{N}_]))#@(\S+)/gu correctly blocks café#@secret.txt and 日本語#@file while matching Review #@a.ts and #@a.ts.
2. INPUT: The test harness file sharp-at-file.test.mjs. Fixture file a.ts exists at TMPDIR. The FIX constant is {cwd: TMPDIR}. The mod.injectFiles function is available.
3. LOGIC: Add a new test case block ('U1' — Unicode boundary) with these assertions:
   (a) 'café#@a.ts' → injectFiles returns injected===0 (é is a Unicode letter; #@ is mid-word; no match).
   (b) '日本語#@a.ts' → injectFiles returns injected===0 (CJK characters are Unicode letters; no match).
   (c) 'Review #@a.ts' → injectFiles returns injected===1 (space before #@; correct match — regression guard).
   (d) '#@a.ts' → injectFiles returns injected===1 (start-of-string; correct match — regression guard).
   (e) 'foo#@bar' → injectFiles returns injected===0 (ASCII mid-word; still correctly blocked — regression guard).
   Each assertion uses mod.injectFiles(input, [], FIX) and checks r.injected and r.text.
4. OUTPUT: The harness has 1 new test case block with 5 assertions. All must pass with the updated FILE_INJECT_RE. Total case count increases by 1.
5. MOCKING: No external services. Uses the same a.ts fixture and injectFiles pattern.
6. DOCS: none — test harness changes have no user-facing documentation surface.

milestones.title: Spec Reconciliation & Changeset-Level Documentation (Issues 3, 4)
milestones.description: Document the intentional F3 (magic-number sniff) and F5 (empty-image handling) deviations from the PRD in the README, and sync all changeset-level documentation (README overview, behavior table, test count, known limitations) to reflect the sentinel removal, Unicode regex fix, and spec reconciliations.

tasks.title: Document intentional F3/F5 deviations in README (Issues 3, 4)
tasks.description: The F3 magic-number sniff and F5 empty-image handling are deliberate improvements over the literal PRD that are not documented. Update the README behavior-by-file-type table and notes to explicitly document these as intentional enhancements, preventing user confusion.

subtasks.title: Document F3 magic-number sniff and F5 empty-image handling in README
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md § 'Spec Deviations'. F3 (hasValidImageMagic at sharp-at-file.ts ~lines 29-52) validates that a file with an image extension has actual image bytes before attaching it as an ImageContent. A mislabeled file (text named .png) falls through to the text/binary path. F5 (formatEmptyImageBlock at ~line 78) special-cases 0-byte images: instead of emitting an empty <file>\n\n</file> (PRD §10), it emits <file name="<abs>"><empty image file — 0 bytes; nothing to attach></file> and attaches no image (an empty ImageContent would be rejected by providers). Both behaviors are verified by the harness (cases F3a, F3b, F5).
2. INPUT: README.md. The behavior-by-file-type table under § 'Behavior by file type' has rows for Text, Image, Other binary, Missing file, Directory, Read/permission error. The known limitations section lists current limitations.
3. LOGIC: Make these README.md changes:
   (a) In the Image row of the behavior-by-file-type table, add a note: image files are validated by BOTH extension AND actual file bytes (magic-number sniff). A file with an image extension but non-image content falls through to the text/binary path. This prevents attaching decoded garbage as an image.
   (b) Add a new row or note for empty image files (0-byte .png/.jpg/etc.): produces <empty image file — 0 bytes; nothing to attach> note block, no image attached. This avoids sending an empty ImageContent that providers would reject.
   (c) In the 'Notes on the table' section below the table, add bullet points explaining both the F3 magic-number validation and F5 empty-image handling as intentional enhancements.
4. OUTPUT: README.md behavior-by-file-type section now documents both the F3 magic-number sniff and F5 empty-image handling. Users reading the README will understand these behaviors are deliberate.
5. MOCKING: No external services. This is pure documentation work.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] This subtask IS the documentation work — it updates README.md § 'Behavior by file type' and § 'Notes on the table' to document the F3/F5 deviations.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (final sweep)
tasks.description: Cross-cutting documentation that only makes sense once all code changes are in place: update the README overview, testing section (pass count), known limitations (remove sentinel references, add Unicode boundary note), and ensure all changes from this bugfix are reflected in the top-level documentation.

subtasks.title: Update README overview, test count, and known limitations
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is the Mode B changeset-level documentation sweep (per SOW §5). It covers cross-cutting docs that span the entire changeset. All implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1, P1.M1.T1.S2, P1.M1.T2.S1, P1.M2.T1.S1, P1.M2.T2.S1, P1.M3.T1.S1) must be complete before this runs.
2. INPUT: README.md as it exists after P1.M1.T2.S1 (Unicode regex Mode A doc update) and P1.M3.T1.S1 (F3/F5 behavior table update). The new total test case count from P1.M2.T1.S1 and P1.M2.T2.S1 (~30-31 cases, was 28).
3. LOGIC: Update README.md with these changeset-level changes:
   (a) Testing section: update the pass count from '28 passed, 0 failed' to the new count (determined by running the updated harness after all code changes). Update the description of what the harness covers to mention co-load dedup, sentinel-in-prompt, and Unicode boundary tests.
   (b) Known limitations section: verify the Unicode-aware word boundary is documented (should already be done by P1.M1.T2.S1's Mode A doc update — if not, add it here). Remove any reference to a sentinel or 'injected' marker in the assembly format.
   (c) Quick start / overview: verify the assembly format description matches the PRD §6.2 format exactly (no sentinel mention). If the README describes the output format, ensure it shows the clean \n\n---\n\n<blocks> format.
   (d) Verify internal consistency: all references to the sentinel mechanism are gone; the dedup is described as path-based, not sentinel-based; the Unicode boundary behavior is documented.
4. OUTPUT: README.md is fully consistent with the implemented code changes. The testing section reflects the actual pass count. No stale references to the sentinel mechanism remain.
5. MOCKING: No external services. Run `node ./sharp-at-file.test.mjs` to get the actual pass count for the testing section.
6. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync — it sweeps README.md for cross-cutting consistency after all implementing subtasks are complete.
